reasoning: Exchange rates generally follow random walks over the medium-to-long term, making the base rate for an increase roughly 0.5. Over the short term, the USD/CNY rate recently experienced a dip from around 7.18 to 7.13 in late August 2025. According to the FRED trend analysis, the actual value (7.13) fell notably below the recent 30-day linear trendline (which projects ~7.155 for early September), supporting a short-term probability > 0.5 due to mean-reversion expectations and the positive differential between US and Chinese interest rates (which conventionally puts upward pressure on the forward rate). Furthermore, mid-2025 outlooks from several investment banks projected end-of-year FX rates in the 7.30-7.50 range, indicating potential structural forces favoring USD strength relative to ~7.13. In accordance with the guidance that uncertainty should increase over longer horizons (pulling distant forecasts closer to 0.5), I estimate the highest probability of an increase at the shortest horizon (as a technical bounce), decaying smoothly to ~0.51 for the 6-month horizon.
